block_01: Will GME stocks repeat its triumph gain?
block_03: For several years in a row, Wall Street hedge funds were selling short stocks of the video games retailer GameStop, believing that its situation would only worsen. Over the past seven years, GME price decreased from $50 to nearly $5 per share, giving an illusion of the company's soon bankruptcy.
block_04: At some point, it seemed that nothing could change the course of events. However, the online investor community on Reddit, r/WallStreetBets, didn't agree. After analyzing the company's books, amateur traders concluded that Wall Street investors unfairly underestimated GME stocks' value and decided to teach them a painful lesson.
block_05: Reddit users really managed to turn things around — the value of GameStop shares at one point rose by more than 14,300%, reaching $469 per stock on January 28, 2021. As a result, hedge funds lost billions of dollars and were forced to close short positions under the threat of possible bankruptcy.
block_08: In April 2020, the hedge fund of Michael Burry (the protagonist of "The Big Short" movie performed by Christian Bale) bought 5.3% of GameStop for $15 million — at prices ranging from $2 to $4.2. By the end of January 2021, that 5.3% were estimated at $1.8 billion.
block_09: Another hedge fund, Senvest Management, has also benefited from the GameStop situation. The fund incorporators went against the market. By the end of October 2020, they owned over 5% of GME shares, most of which were bought for $10 per stock. This resulted in the fund's portfolio growth from $1.6 billion to $ 2.4 billion at the end of 2020.
block_11: spoke out about the GameStop situation
block_12: on Twitter. The businessman supported the Reddit community and expressed his contempt for short-sellers. As a result of his actions on social media, GME shares' value on the premarket rose by more than 40%.
block_15: Since the beginning of February, GameStop shares began to fall — by February 9, it has already dropped to $50 per share, returning to more or less standard indicators. However, Wall Street officials have yet to investigate the GameStop precedent more deeply since the story might reoccur in the future.
